sourceMovie.title: Blade Runner
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Blade Runner works as a science fiction movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The science fiction and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Terminator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Blade Runner clicked for you, The Terminator (1984) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Blade Runner clicked for you, Insurgent (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Blade Runner?
faq.answer: Try The Terminator, Insurgent, Cloud Atlas—each shares DNA with Blade Runner while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Blade Runner?
faq.answer: Blade Runner (1982) is best described as Science Fiction, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Blade Runner part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Blade Runner Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
